ADDENDUM TO PURCHASE CONTRACT
V alencia H omes · Price & Terms rider
This Addendum to Purchase Contract (this “Addendum”) is made a part of, and incorporated into, that certain Residential Purchase
Contract No. 291639 dated 12/01/2023 (the “Contract”) by and between the Buyer and Seller identified above, covering the real
property described above. In the event of any conflict between the Contract and this Addendum, the terms of this Addendum shall
control.
ARTICLE 1 — PURCHASE PRICE AND TERMS
A. The parties confirm and restate the total purchase price for the Property, together with the price components and selections/options
agreed to under the Contract, as set forth in the schedule below. All amounts are stated in U.S. Dollars.
Base Purchase Price $772,965.00
Lot Premium $0.00
Selections / Options Total $0.00
Total Purchase Price $772,965.00
B. The financing contemplated under the Contract is a mortgage loan in the principal amount of $394,212.00. After application of the
loan proceeds and all deposits received (set forth in Article 2 below), the estimated balance due from Buyer at closing is
$305,363.00, subject to final settlement adjustments and prorations.
ARTICLE 2 — DEPOSIT SCHEDULE
C. Buyer has made the deposits itemized below, the aggregate of which is credited against the Total Purchase Price at closing. Each
deposit is held in accordance with the Contract.

NOTICE & DISCLOSURE
This Consumer Account Terms and Conditions Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into by and between Kyle Calderon ("you" or "Borrower") and
Guaranteed Rate, Inc. ("we," "us," "our," or "Lender") in connection with the account, loan, or credit facility identified by loan number 2486187376 (the
"Account"). By accepting funds, signing the related note or disclosure, or otherwise using the Account on or after February 11, 2024, you acknowledge that you
have read, understood, and agreed to be bound by every provision set forth herein. This Agreement supplements and is incorporated by reference into your
promissory note, security instrument, and any related disclosures, and it governs the rights and obligations of the parties throughout the life of the Account.
§ 1. DEFINITIONS
As used throughout this Agreement, certain capitalized terms carry the meanings assigned to them in this section unless the context plainly requires otherwise. "Account"
means the credit facility, loan, or line of credit established between you and Guaranteed Rate, Inc. and identified by the loan number referenced above. "Business Day"
means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday, or federal banking holiday on which our offices are open for the conduct of substantially all of our ordinary business.
"Servicer" means the entity that we designate from time to time to administer billing, collection, and customer-service functions, which may be Guaranteed Rate, Inc. or
any successor that we appoint in accordance with applicable law.
"Indebtedness" means the aggregate of all amounts that you owe to us under this Agreement and the related loan documents, including outstanding principal, accrued and
unpaid interest, late charges, returned-item fees, and any other sums properly assessed under the governing instruments. "Loan Documents" means, collectively, your
promissory note, this Agreement, any security instrument, and every disclosure, rider, addendum, or amendment executed in connection with the Account. "Applicable
Law" means all federal, PA state, and local statutes, regulations, rules, and ordinances, together with all binding judicial and administrative interpretations thereof, as the
same may be amended or supplemented from time to time during the term of the Account.
§ 2. PROMISE TO PAY
You unconditionally promise to pay to the order of Guaranteed Rate, Inc., or to any subsequent holder of the Loan Documents, the full amount of the Indebtedness in
accordance with the payment terms disclosed to you, together with interest accruing on the unpaid principal balance at the rate and in the manner specified in your
promissory note. Your obligation to pay is absolute and is not subject to any condition, counterclaim, setoff, or deduction except as expressly permitted by Applicable Law.
Each payment must be made in lawful money of the United States and received by us at the address designated for that purpose or through any electronic channel we have
authorized.
If more than one person signs the Loan Documents as a borrower, each such person agrees to be jointly and severally liable for the entire Indebtedness, meaning that we
may enforce this Agreement against any one of you, against some of you, or against all of you together, and we may do so without first proceeding against any other
obligor, guarantor, or collateral. The release or modification of the obligations of any one borrower shall not impair the liability of any remaining borrower. You remain
obligated to perform notwithstanding any extension, indulgence, or accommodation that we may extend to any other party at any time.
§ 3. APPLICATION OF PAYMENTS
Unless Applicable Law requires a different order, we will apply each payment we receive and accept first to any accrued and unpaid interest, then to outstanding principal,
and thereafter to escrow obligations, late charges, and other fees lawfully assessed against the Account, in the sequence we determine in our reasonable discretion.
Payments received after the daily or monthly cutoff that we establish may be credited as of the next Business Day. We are not obligated to accept any partial payment, and
our acceptance of a partial or late payment shall not constitute a waiver of our right to require payment in full or to declare a default for any amount that remains unpaid.
If you direct that a payment be applied in a particular manner, or if you tender a payment marked "paid in full" or accompanied by any restrictive endorsement or condition,
we may accept and process that payment without being bound by the stated restriction, and no such notation shall operate as an accord and satisfaction unless we expressly
agree in a separate writing signed by an authorized officer. Funds tendered toward future installments do not relieve you of the obligation to make each scheduled payment
as it becomes due, nor do they change the due date or the amount of any subsequent installment.
§ 4. LATE CHARGES AND FEES
If any scheduled payment is not received in full by the end of the grace period disclosed in your Loan Documents, you agree to pay a late charge in the amount and at the
rate permitted by Applicable Law and set forth in those documents. A late charge is assessed to compensate us for the administrative cost and inconvenience of handling a
delinquent payment and does not constitute interest, a penalty, or our consent to late payment. We may assess such a charge for each delinquent installment, and the
imposition of one or more late charges shall not limit any other right or remedy otherwise available to us under this Agreement.
You further agree to reimburse us for any fee that we incur or that is permitted under Applicable Law in connection with a check, draft, or electronic payment that is
returned or dishonored for any reason, as well as for charges associated with property inspections, payoff statements, and similar services lawfully provided in the servicing
of your Account. All fees properly assessed and remaining unpaid become part of the Indebtedness and may accrue interest at the rate applicable to principal. We will
disclose the nature and amount of any such fee in the manner required by Applicable Law before or at the time it is charged to the Account.
§ 5. DEFAULT
You will be in default under this Agreement upon the occurrence of any of the following events: your failure to make any payment when due; your failure to perform any
other material obligation set forth in the Loan Documents; the discovery that any representation or warranty you made was materially false or misleading when made; your
insolvency, assignment for the benefit of creditors, or the commencement of any voluntary or involuntary bankruptcy proceeding affecting you; or the levy, seizure, or
attachment of any collateral securing the Account. Default also occurs if any other event identified as an event of default in your promissory note or security instrument
takes place.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

Except where Applicable Law provides otherwise or where the nature of the default makes notice or cure impracticable, we will give you any notice of default and any
right to cure that Applicable Law requires before we accelerate the Indebtedness or pursue remedies. A notice of default will, to the extent required, specify the nature of
the default, the action necessary to cure it, a date by which the cure must be completed, and the consequences of failing to cure within the stated period. Our failure to
declare a default upon the occurrence of one event shall not constitute a waiver of our right to declare a default upon any subsequent or continuing event.
§ 6. REMEDIES UPON DEFAULT
Upon the occurrence of a default that remains uncured beyond any applicable cure period, we may, at our option and to the extent permitted by Applicable Law, declare the
entire unpaid Indebtedness, together with all accrued interest and lawfully assessed charges, immediately due and payable without further demand or notice, all of which
you hereby waive to the fullest extent the law allows. We may thereafter exercise any and all rights and remedies available to us under the Loan Documents, at law, or in
equity, including the right to enforce any security interest, to foreclose upon collateral, and to pursue collection of any deficiency that remains after the application of
proceeds.
All of our rights and remedies are cumulative and not exclusive, and we may exercise them concurrently, successively, or in any order we choose. No delay or omission on
our part in exercising any right or remedy shall impair that right or remedy or be construed as a waiver thereof. You agree to pay, to the extent permitted by Applicable
Law, the reasonable costs and expenses we incur in enforcing this Agreement, including court costs and reasonable attorneys' fees, whether incurred before, during, or after
any judicial or bankruptcy proceeding, and such amounts shall be added to the Indebtedness.
§ 7. CHANGES TO TERMS
We may amend, modify, or supplement the terms of this Agreement from time to time to the extent permitted by Applicable Law and by your Loan Documents. Where a
change requires your advance notice or consent, we will provide notice in the form, manner, and within the timeframe that Applicable Law prescribes, which may include
written notice mailed to the most recent address reflected in our records or, where you have consented, an electronic communication. Certain terms, including a fixed
contractual interest rate, may not be altered except as expressly authorized by the Loan Documents or as you and we may agree in a signed writing.
If a change in law or regulation requires us to modify any provision of this Agreement, that provision shall be deemed amended to the minimum extent necessary to bring it
into compliance, and the remainder of the Agreement shall continue in full force. Your continued use of the Account, or your failure to terminate or pay off the Account
following an effective change to which you have a right to object, where such conduct constitutes acceptance under Applicable Law, shall be deemed your agreement to the
change. No amendment proposed by you shall be binding upon us unless accepted in writing by an authorized officer of Guaranteed Rate, Inc..
§ 8. GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement and all rights and obligations arising under it shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of PA and, where applicable, the
laws of the United States, without giving effect to any conflict-of-laws principle that would require the application of the law of another jurisdiction. To the extent that
federal law preempts or supplements state law with respect to any provision, federal law shall control as to that provision. The location of the property or collateral, where
relevant, may further determine the body of law applicable to the enforcement of any security interest, and nothing in this section shall be read to diminish any protection
afforded to you by mandatory provisions of Applicable Law.
You agree that any action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement may be brought in the state or federal courts located in or serving the jurisdiction in
which the Account is administered or the collateral is situated, and you submit to the personal jurisdiction of such courts to the extent permitted by Applicable Law.
Nothing in this section limits any right you may have under Applicable Law to bring or defend a proceeding in another permissible venue, nor does it waive any protection
that cannot be waived under the consumer-protection laws of PA.
§ 9. SEVERABILITY
If any provision of this Agreement, or the application of any provision to any person or circumstance, is held by a court or other tribunal of competent jurisdiction to be
invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, that determination shall not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full
force and effect as though the offending provision had never been included. The parties intend that each provision of this Agreement be severable, and that the Agreement
be enforced to the maximum extent permitted by Applicable Law notwithstanding the partial invalidity of any term.
Where a provision is found to be unenforceable only because it exceeds a limit imposed by Applicable Law, that provision shall be deemed reduced or reformed to the
maximum extent permitted by law and enforced as so modified, in order to give effect, to the greatest degree possible, to the original intent of the parties. In particular, if
any charge collected or contracted for under this Agreement is determined to exceed a lawful maximum, the excess shall be applied to reduce principal or, if there is no
principal outstanding, refunded to you in the manner required by Applicable Law.
§ 10. ASSIGNMENT
We may sell, assign, transfer, pledge, or otherwise convey this Agreement, the Loan Documents, the Account, or any of our rights and obligations under them, in whole or
in part and at any time, to any person or entity without notice to you except as Applicable Law may require, and without your consent. Following any such transfer, the
assignee shall succeed to our rights and, to the extent of the assignment, may exercise every right and remedy available to us hereunder. We may also delegate the servicing
of the Account to a Servicer at any time, and you agree to remit payments and direct inquiries as the current Servicer directs.
You may not assign, delegate, or transfer your rights or obligations under this Agreement to any other person without our prior written consent, and any purported
assignment by you made without such consent shall be void and of no effect. The sale or transfer of any property securing the Account does not relieve you of liability for
the Indebtedness unless we expressly agree in writing to release you. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of you and us and our respective heirs,
executors, administrators, successors, and permitted assigns.
§ 11. NOTICES
Except where Applicable Law specifies a different method, any notice that you are required or permitted to give us under this Agreement must be in writing and delivered
to Guaranteed Rate, Inc. at 9768 Jefferson Park, Leslieton, GU 54749, or to such other address as we may designate in a subsequent notice, and shall be deemed given
when actually received by us. Any notice that we are required or permitted to give you may be delivered by first-class mail, postage prepaid, addressed to you at 739
Christine Alley Suite 376, Randallborough, PA 66679 or at the most recent address shown in our records, and shall be deemed given on the date of mailing unless
Applicable Law requires actual receipt.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

If more than one person is obligated under the Account, notice given to any one of you shall constitute notice to all of you for purposes of this Agreement. You agree to
notify us promptly in writing of any change in your mailing address, telephone number, or other contact information, and you remain responsible for ensuring that we have
current information through which to reach you. Where you have consented to receive communications electronically, we may provide notices and disclosures by
electronic means to the extent permitted by Applicable Law, and such delivery shall have the same effect as delivery by mail. You may reach us by telephone at
326-955-0123.
§ 12. NO WAIVER
No failure or delay on our part in exercising any right, power, or privilege under this Agreement, and no course of dealing between you and us, shall operate as a waiver of
that right, power, or privilege, nor shall any single or partial exercise of any right preclude any further exercise of that right or the exercise of any other right. Any waiver of
any provision of this Agreement, or of any default, must be in writing and signed by an authorized officer of Guaranteed Rate, Inc. to be effective, and any such waiver
shall be limited to the specific provision or default expressly identified.
Our acceptance of one or more late, partial, or irregular payments, our extension of time for performance, our forbearance from exercising any remedy, or our agreement to
any single accommodation shall not constitute a waiver of our right to insist upon strict compliance with the terms of this Agreement thereafter, nor shall it establish a
custom or course of dealing that varies those terms. To the extent permitted by Applicable Law, you waive presentment, demand for payment, notice of dishonor, protest,
and notice of protest with respect to the Indebtedness.
§ 13. ENTIRE AGREEMENT
This Agreement, together with your promissory note, security instrument, and the other Loan Documents, constitutes the entire agreement between you and Guaranteed
Rate, Inc. with respect to the Account and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous understandings, representations, negotiations, and agreements, whether oral or written,
relating to its subject matter. You acknowledge that you have not relied upon any statement, promise, or representation that is not expressly set forth in the Loan
Documents, and that no oral statement by any of our representatives shall be deemed to modify or supplement the written terms.
In the event of any direct conflict between the provisions of this Agreement and those of another Loan Document, the documents shall be read together and harmonized to
the greatest extent possible; where harmonization is not possible, the provision affording you the greater protection under Applicable Law, or the provision specifically
governing the matter in question, shall control. The headings used in this Agreement are inserted for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the interpretation of
any provision. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which together shall constitute one and the same
instrument, effective as of February 11, 2024.

Privacy Notice  l Lender  Convenient Rate, Inc.  Loan No.  12-34567  Date  8/30/2023  Kyle Callahan  NOTICE & DISCLOSURE  This Privacy Notice describes how Convenient Rate, Inc., ("Convenient Rate", "we", "us", "our") collects, shares, and protects the personal information of customers. The information is required by the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act, 15 U.S.C.  6801 et seq.  Financial companies choose how they share your personal information. When you apply for a loan or open an account, we may collect and share this information with others as permitted by law. This notice explains the choices you have to limit sharing. This notice is effective for the 12 months after it is initially provided to you.

1. WHY WE PROVIDE THIS NOTICE  Financial companies choose how they share personal information, and federal law, including the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act and its implementing regulations, requires us to tell you how we collect, share, and protect your information. We are providing this notice to you because you are a customer of ours. This notice applies to you whether you are applying for a loan, a credit card, or another product. We want you to know that we value and respect your privacy.

2. INFORMATION WE COLLECT  We collect personal information about you in connection with providing you a financial product or service. The information we collect depends on the product or service you receive from us and may include the following:  - Social Security number   - Income   - Credit history  - Credit score  - Account balances and transaction history, including those of affiliated companies.

3. HOW WE SHARE YOUR INFORMATION  All financial companies need to share customers’ personal information to run their everyday business. In the sections that follow, we describe the reasons financial companies can share their customers’ personal information; the reasons we choose to share; and whether you can limit this sharing.  We share information with other financial service providers, including affiliated companies.  We also share information when we are complying with our legal obligations.

4. REASONS YOU CANNOT LIMIT SHARING  We reveal certain information that we have to; for example, if you refinance or apply for a loan or other types of products, which includes providing your information to affiliates or others to process or evaluate applications.

5. SHARING WITH AFFILIATES  Affiliates are financial institutions that we own or come under common control. We may share with our affiliates information about your transactions, and all transactions we perform on your behalf, including types of documents processed, data processing, etc.

We may also share with our affiliates certain other information about you, including information or extracts from your application and from consumer reporting agencies that you have authorized. Federal law gives you the right to limit sharing of certain types of information about you with our affiliates in that capacity. Even if you limit sharing, we may continue to share with our affiliates in situations permitted by law, such as to facilitate your transaction or service your account.

Any choices you make do not limit our ability to share information as described in this notice unless you specifically tell us to limit sharing among our affiliates or unless otherwise required by law.

5. SHARING WITH NONAFFILIATES

We do not share personal information with nonaffiliates except as permitted by law.

6. JOINT MARKETING ARRANGEMENTS

As your marketing partner, we may enter into a formal agreement between financial companies that together market financial products or services to you. (Commonly known as "joint marketing"). If we go forward, each party will be responsible for the financial products and services it provides to you to clearly let you know how to reach either party regarding your financial product or service. No naming will be used regarding their relationship unless it is appropriate and meets all compliance requirements.
  
If you do not want us to share your information for marketing financial products and services with nonaffiliated financial companies, you may contact us in writing and state your specific requests. 

7. YOUR CHOICES AND HOW TO LIMIT SHARING

If you are not a current customer and wish to limit sharing as described in this notice, we have listed several options for you. When you are no longer a customer, you still have the ability to limit sharing. You can tell us your choices by contacting us using the information provided at the end of this notice.

If you are our customer, you may choose to limit our sharing by contacting us. You can also receive information about your choices regarding how we may share information with our affiliates (e.g. to inform you about financial products, loans, debit cards, ATMs, etc.) when you first become a customer with us.

If you wish to limit our sharing, please choose one of the following actions:

1. Contact us using the toll-free number for your account holders, use your number to identify your account.
2. If you are an online user, you may send us a secure email.
3. Complete a service request in your customer portal.

If you wish to limit sharing, you must specify your request and describe which of the account holders you wish to exclude or include in your request. 

8. HOW WE PROTECT YOUR INFORMATION

To protect your personal information, we use security measures that maintain physical, electronic, and procedural safeguards that comply with applicable federal law. We use encryption and firewalls to protect your data and restrict access to your records. 

We limit access to your information to employees, agents, and service providers who have a legitimate business need for that information. We give our employees the tools and guidelines to help them protect your information.

9. INFORMATION FOR FORMER CUSTOMERS

If you are or have been a customer, you have the same rights and abilities to control how we share your information with nonaffiliated third parties as you did when you were still a customer.

10. CHANGES TO THIS NOTICE AND HOW TO CONTACT US

We reserve the right to amend this Privacy Notice at any time to reflect changes in our information practices, as well as applicable laws. When necessary, we will notify you by mail, and also by posting the changes on our website, depending on the sensitivity of the changes made.

GUARANTEED RATE, INC. — CONSUMER ACCOUNT TERMS AND CONDITIONS
AGREEMENT
Consumer Account Terms and Conditions
Issued to Kyle Calderon

LENDER Guaranteed Rate, Inc.
LOAN NO. 2486187376
DATE 02/11/2024
BORROWER Kyle Calderon

This Consumer Account Terms and Conditions Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into by and between Kyle Calderon ("you" or "Borrower") and
Guaranteed Rate, Inc. ("we," "us," "our," or "Lender") in connection with the account, loan, or credit facility identified by loan number 2486187376 (the
"Account"). By accepting funds, signing the related note or disclosure, or otherwise using the Account on or after February 11, 2024, you acknowledge that you
have read, understood, and agreed to be bound by every provision set forth herein. This Agreement supplements and is incorporated by reference into your
promissory note, security instrument, and any related disclosures, and it governs the rights and obligations of the parties throughout the life of the Account.
§ 1. DEFINITIONS
As used throughout this Agreement, certain capitalized terms carry the meanings assigned to them in this section unless the context plainly requires otherwise. "Account"
means the credit facility, loan, or line of credit established between you and Guaranteed Rate, Inc. and identified by the loan number referenced above. "Business Day"
means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday, or federal banking holiday on which our offices are open for the conduct of substantially all of our ordinary business.
"Servicer" means the entity that we designate from time to time to administer billing, collection, and customer-service functions, which may be Guaranteed Rate, Inc. or
any successor that we appoint in accordance with applicable law.
"Indebtedness" means the aggregate of all amounts that you owe to us under this Agreement and the related loan documents, including outstanding principal, accrued and
unpaid interest, late charges, returned-item fees, and any other sums properly assessed under the governing instruments. "Loan Documents" means, collectively, your
promissory note, this Agreement, any security instrument, and every disclosure, rider, addendum, or amendment executed in connection with the Account. "Applicable
Law" means all federal, PA state, and local statutes, regulations, rules, and ordinances, together with all binding judicial and administrative interpretations thereof, as the
same may be amended or supplemented from time to time during the term of the Account.
§ 2. PROMISE TO PAY
You unconditionally promise to pay to the order of Guaranteed Rate, Inc., or to any subsequent holder of the Loan Documents, the full amount of the Indebtedness in
accordance with the payment terms disclosed to you, together with interest accruing on the unpaid principal balance at the rate and in the manner specified in your
promissory note. Your obligation to pay is absolute and is not subject to any condition, counterclaim, setoff, or deduction except as expressly permitted by Applicable Law.
Each payment must be made in lawful money of the United States and received by us at the address designated for that purpose or through any electronic channel we have
authorized.
If more than one person signs the Loan Documents as a borrower, each such person agrees to be jointly and severally liable for the entire Indebtedness, meaning that we
may enforce this Agreement against any one of you, against some of you, or against all of you together, and we may do so without first proceeding against any other
obligor, guarantor, or collateral. The release or modification of the obligations of any one borrower shall not impair the liability of any remaining borrower. You remain
obligated to perform notwithstanding any extension, indulgence, or accommodation that we may extend to any other party at any time.
§ 3. APPLICATION OF PAYMENTS
Unless Applicable Law requires a different order, we will apply each payment we receive and accept first to any accrued and unpaid interest, then to outstanding principal,
and thereafter to escrow obligations, late charges, and other fees lawfully assessed against the Account, in the sequence we determine in our reasonable discretion.
Payments received after the daily or monthly cutoff that we establish may be credited as of the next Business Day. We are not obligated to accept any partial payment, and
our acceptance of a partial or late payment shall not constitute a waiver of our right to require payment in full or to declare a default for any amount that remains unpaid.
If you direct that a payment be applied in a particular manner, or if you tender a payment marked "paid in full" or accompanied by any restrictive endorsement or condition,
we may accept and process that payment without being bound by the stated restriction, and no such notation shall operate as an accord and satisfaction unless we expressly
agree in a separate writing signed by an authorized officer. Funds tendered toward future installments do not relieve you of the obligation to make each scheduled payment
as it becomes due, nor do they change the due date or the amount of any subsequent installment.
§ 4. LATE CHARGES AND FEES
If any scheduled payment is not received in full by the end of the grace period disclosed in your Loan Documents, you agree to pay a late charge in the amount and at the
rate permitted by Applicable Law and set forth in those documents. A late charge is assessed to compensate us for the administrative cost and inconvenience of handling a
delinquent payment and does not constitute interest, a penalty, or our consent to late payment. We may assess such a charge for each delinquent installment, and the
imposition of one or more late charges shall not limit any other right or remedy otherwise available to us under this Agreement.
You further agree to reimburse us for any fee that we incur or that is permitted under Applicable Law in connection with a check, draft, or electronic payment that is
returned or dishonored for any reason, as well as for charges associated with property inspections, payoff statements, and similar services lawfully provided in the servicing
of your Account. All fees properly assessed and remaining unpaid become part of the Indebtedness and may accrue interest at the rate applicable to principal. We will
disclose the nature and amount of any such fee in the manner required by Applicable Law before or at the time it is charged to the Account.
§ 5. DEFAULT
You will be in default under this Agreement upon the occurrence of any of the following events: your failure to make any payment when due; your failure to perform any
other material obligation set forth in the Loan Documents; the discovery that any representation or warranty you made was materially false or misleading when made; your
insolvency, assignment for the benefit of creditors, or the commencement of any voluntary or involuntary bankruptcy proceeding affecting you; or the levy, seizure, or
attachment of any collateral securing the Account. Default also occurs if any other event identified as an event of default in your promissory note or security instrument
takes place.
